steps.title: Undo the coefficient
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $-\frac{15}{4}$:
$$-\frac{15}{4}(D-T)=100-H$$
The left side can also be written as $\frac{15}{4}(T-D)$.

steps.title: Solve for H
steps.content:
Subtract $100$ from both sides and then multiply by $-1$:
$$H=100+\frac{15}{4}(D-T)$$
So the equivalent expression is:
$$H=\frac{15}{4}(D-T)+100$$

steps.title: Check the answer choice with sample values
steps.content:
Use quick sample values to catch sign errors. If $T=82$ and $H=70$, then the original formula gives $D=82-\frac{4}{15}(30)=74$. Plugging $D=74$ and $T=82$ into the correct expression gives:
$$\frac{15}{4}(74-82)+100=70$$
It returns the original $H$, so A is correct.
